executive_summary.recommended_approach: Strangler fig modernization: wrap IBMi RPG programs with REST API layer, build responsive React web UI, implement modern IAM with SSO/MFA — phased module-by-module rollout preserving all existing business logic.
key_benefits: 90% reduction in training time — modern UIs eliminate terminal navigation learning curve
HIPAA compliance — SSO, MFA, RBAC, and comprehensive PHI audit trails close current audit findings
Mobile access — clinical staff can use tablets/phones across all 3 facilities
Recruitment advantage — modern tech stack attracts talent vs. green screen environment
API-first architecture enables future EHR integrations and AI capabilities
Risk mitigation — IBMi business logic preserved, no rewrite risk
key_risks: IBMi integration complexity — EBCDIC/packed decimal conversion, connection pool management, RPG program interfaces require specialized expertise
Change management — clinical staff resistant after 15+ years on same system; phased rollout and training program critical
RPG developer dependency — only 2 developers, both near retirement; knowledge transfer must be prioritized
HIPAA compliance scope — PHI data throughout all modules increases security implementation effort
Legacy JD Edwards integration — file-based transfer is fragile; needs monitoring and error handling

frontend.framework: React 18 with Next.js 14 (SSR for performance, accessibility)
frontend.rationale: React ecosystem has largest talent pool, Next.js provides SSR for fast initial load and SEO. Responsive design with Tailwind CSS for mobile support. Strong accessibility tooling (react-aria, axe-core integration).

primary.purpose: All patient, clinical, billing, and operational data. Accessed via IBMi Data Access Layer.

session_cache.purpose: Session management, token caching, API response caching for read-heavy queries.

audit_store.hosting: HIPAA-compliant cloud with encryption at rest
audit_store.purpose: Immutable audit log for PHI access events, authentication events, and data modifications. HIPAA compliance requirement.

user_directory.purpose: User accounts, role mappings, session metadata. Synced with external IdP.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (HIPAA BAA available) or Azure (HIPAA BAA available)
infrastructure.compute: ECS Fargate (serverless containers) for application tier; IBM AS/400 on-premises for backend
infrastructure.container_orchestration: ECS Fargate (managed, no cluster management overhead)
infrastructure.logging: CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON logging, 7-year retention for HIPAA audit logs

component_design.name: Web Application (React/Next.js)
component_design.purpose: Responsive web UI replacing green screen terminals. Serves all 6 clinical modules plus admin dashboards. WCAG 2.1 AA compliant.
component_design.scalability: Horizontal — CDN for static assets, SSR instances scale with user count

component_design.name: API Gateway
component_design.purpose: Central entry point for all client requests. Routes to backend services, enforces authentication, rate limiting, and request validation.

component_design.name: IBMi Data Access Layer (DAL)
component_design.technology: Node.js, IBM i Access ODBC/JDBC drivers, node-jt400
component_design.purpose: Abstraction layer for all IBMi communication. Handles connection pooling, EBCDIC conversion, RPG program calls, and DB2/400 queries. Facade pattern over legacy interfaces.
component_design.scalability: Limited by IBMi connection pool (50 concurrent). Vertical scaling with caching at C-006.

component_design.name: IAM / Authentication Service
component_design.technology: Keycloak (open-source IdP) or AWS Cognito, SAML 2.0, OIDC
component_design.purpose: Single sign-on, multi-factor authentication, role-based access control, session management. Integrates with client's identity provider. Maps clinical roles to application permissions.
component_design.scalability: Horizontal — stateless token validation, session state in C-006

component_design.name: Business Logic Services
component_design.technology: NestJS microservices (Patient, Scheduling, Billing, Pharmacy, Lab, ClinDoc)
component_design.purpose: Domain-specific service modules mapping modern API contracts to IBMi backend operations. Each module encapsulates one green screen application's functionality.
component_design.scalability: Horizontal per module — independent scaling based on module traffic

component_design.name: Cache Layer (Redis)
component_design.technology: Redis 7, ElastiCache (AWS) or Azure Cache for Redis
component_design.purpose: Session storage, API response caching for read-heavy queries, token validation cache. Reduces load on IBMi connection pool.
inputs: Cache reads/writes from C-002, C-004, C-005
component_design.scalability: Horizontal — Redis Cluster with read replicas

component_design.name: Audit Logging Service
component_design.purpose: Captures all PHI access events, authentication events, and data modifications. Async write via message queue to avoid blocking application flow. Immutable storage for HIPAA compliance.
inputs: Audit events from all services via message queue
component_design.scalability: Horizontal — queue-based ingestion handles burst, PostgreSQL partitioned by date

component_design.name: Monitoring & Observability
component_design.purpose: Centralized monitoring of all components. Health checks, performance metrics, error rate tracking, IBMi connectivity monitoring, SLA dashboards.
component_design.scalability: Vertical — single monitoring stack scales with metric volume

data_flows.name: Auth Flow: External IdP → IAM Service
All Services → Audit Logger
data_flows.encryption: TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest

system_context.code:
graph TB
    Users["Clinical & Admin Staff\n(500+ users, 3 facilities)"]
    IdP["External Identity Provider\n(SAML/OIDC)"]
    IBMi["IBM AS/400\n(RPG, DB2/400, COBOL)"]
    JDE["JD Edwards\n(Financial)"]
    
    subgraph ModernPlatform["Modern Application Platform"]
        WebUI["Web Application\n(React/Next.js)"]
        APIGw["API Gateway\n(NestJS)"]
        IAM["IAM Service\n(Keycloak/Cognito)"]
        BizLogic["Business Logic\nServices"]
        DAL["IBMi Data Access Layer"]
        AuditSvc["Audit Service"]
        Cache["Redis Cache"]
        Monitor["Monitoring\n(Prometheus/Grafana)"]
    end
    
    Users -->|HTTPS| WebUI
    WebUI -->|REST API| APIGw
    APIGw -->|Auth| IAM
    IAM <-->|SAML/OIDC| IdP
    APIGw --> BizLogic
    BizLogic --> DAL
    DAL <-->|ODBC/JDBC| IBMi
    BizLogic --> Cache
    BizLogic --> AuditSvc
    APIGw --> AuditSvc
    JDE -.->|File Transfer| BizLogic
    Monitor -.-> ModernPlatform

deployment_view.code:
graph TB
    subgraph Client["Client Network"]
        IBMi["IBM AS/400\n(On-Premises)"]
        IdP["Identity Provider"]
    end
    
    subgraph Cloud["HIPAA-Compliant Cloud (AWS/Azure)"]
        subgraph AZ1["Availability Zone 1"]
            ALB1["Application Load Balancer"]
            ECS1["ECS Fargate\n(App Containers)"]
            Redis1["Redis Primary"]
        end
        subgraph AZ2["Availability Zone 2"]
            ALB2["ALB (Failover)"]
            ECS2["ECS Fargate\n(App Containers)"]
            Redis2["Redis Replica"]
        end
        PG["PostgreSQL RDS\n(Audit + Users)"]
        SQS["SQS\n(Audit Queue)"]
        WAF["WAF + Shield"]
        CW["CloudWatch"]
    end
    
    Users["End Users"] -->|HTTPS| WAF
    WAF --> ALB1
    ALB1 --> ECS1
    ECS1 <-->|VPN/Direct Connect| IBMi
    ECS1 --> Redis1
    ECS1 --> SQS
    SQS --> PG
    Redis1 -.-> Redis2
    ECS1 -.-> ECS2
    ECS1 <-->|SAML| IdP
    CW -.-> Cloud

data_flow.code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User (Browser)
    participant W as Web App (React)
    participant A as API Gateway
    participant Auth as IAM Service
    participant BL as Business Logic
    participant DAL as IBMi DAL
    participant DB as DB2/400
    participant Aud as Audit Service
    
    U->>W: Navigate to patient registration
    W->>A: GET /api/v1/patients?search=...
    A->>Auth: Validate JWT token
    Auth-->>A: Token valid, roles: [nurse]
    A->>BL: Forward request with user context
    BL->>DAL: Query patient by criteria
    DAL->>DB: ODBC call to RPG program PTLOOKUP
    DB-->>DAL: EBCDIC result set
    DAL-->>BL: JSON patient records
    BL->>Aud: Log PHI access event (async)
    BL-->>A: Patient list response
    A-->>W: JSON response
    W-->>U: Render patient list UI

services.compute: ECS Fargate (serverless containers — no EC2 management)
services.networking: VPC with private subnets, VPN/Direct Connect to IBMi on-premises

cloud_infrastructure.deployment_pattern: Blue-green via ECS with CodeDeploy. Zero-downtime deployments with automatic rollback on health check failure.

disaster_recovery.strategy: Multi-AZ deployment within single region. PostgreSQL RDS with automated backups and point-in-time recovery. Redis replication across AZs. IBMi has its own DR (client-managed).

cost_optimization.compute: Fargate Savings Plans for predictable baseline, auto-scaling for peak
cost_optimization.storage: S3 lifecycle policies for audit log archival (Standard → IA → Glacier after 1 year)
cost_optimization.monitoring: CloudWatch log retention tiered by importance (7 days debug, 7 years audit)

ai_ml_components.current_phase: None — this is a UI modernization engagement. AI/ML identified as future phase only.

future_considerations.ai_assisted_data_entry: NLP-powered form auto-fill from clinical notes (Phase 3+)
future_considerations.clinical_decision_support: Drug interaction checking enhancement with ML models (Phase 3+)
future_considerations.natural_language_search: Patient record search via natural language queries (Phase 3+)

logging.format: Structured JSON (timestamp, level, service, correlation_id, message, context)
logging.aggregation: CloudWatch Logs with log group per service
logging.retention: 7 days for debug logs, 90 days for application logs, 7 years for audit/PHI access logs (HIPAA)
logging.phi_protection: PHI fields masked in application logs, full detail only in audit logs

metrics.application: Request count, latency (P50/P95/P99), error rate, active sessions
metrics.ibmi_specific: Connection pool utilization, ODBC query latency, RPG program response time
metrics.business: Active users, transactions per module, PHI access count

tracing.scope: Request-level tracing from Web UI through API Gateway to IBMi DAL
tracing.correlation: Correlation ID propagated through all service calls and audit events

notes.operational_excellence: Strong: CI/CD pipeline, structured logging, monitoring. Gap: IBMi-specific runbooks needed, RPG developer knowledge transfer documentation.
notes.security: Strong: Zero Trust (SSO, MFA, RBAC), HIPAA compliance, PHI audit trails, encryption everywhere. Gap: Penetration testing plan needed before go-live.
notes.reliability: Strong: Multi-AZ deployment, circuit breakers, retry patterns. Gap: IBMi is single point of failure (client-managed DR), connection pool limits constrain burst capacity.
notes.performance_efficiency: Strong: Redis caching reduces IBMi load, CDN for static assets. Gap: IBMi ODBC latency may be bottleneck for complex queries; need performance testing with real data volumes.
notes.cost_optimization: Strong: Fargate (pay-per-use), caching strategy, storage lifecycle. Gap: IBMi licensing costs not included (client-owned), need to validate Fargate vs EC2 cost crossover point.
notes.sustainability: Moderate: Serverless compute is efficient, caching reduces redundant processing. Gap: On-premises IBMi power consumption not addressed, no carbon-aware scheduling.

alternatives_considered.name: Full IBMi replacement (rip-and-replace with cloud-native)
alternatives_considered.summary: Replace all IBMi applications with cloud-native microservices and modern database
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Unacceptable risk for healthcare organization. 15+ years of business logic in RPG programs would need to be rewritten — high defect risk, 3-5x budget, 2-3x timeline. Zero tolerance for data loss with 2M+ patient records. Strangler fig preserves proven logic while modernizing UI.

alternatives_considered.name: Commercial IBMi screen scraping (e.g., Rocket BlueZone, Fresche Presto)
alternatives_considered.summary: Use commercial tools that auto-generate web UIs from existing green screens
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Screen scraping produces UIs that mirror green screen workflows — does not achieve HCD goals. No mobile optimization, limited accessibility compliance. Does not address IAM requirements (SSO, MFA, RBAC). Vendor lock-in with ongoing licensing costs. Does not provide API layer for future integrations.

alternatives_considered.name: Angular instead of React for frontend
alternatives_considered.summary: Use Angular framework for the web application
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Smaller talent pool in client's region. Angular's opinionated structure is good but React ecosystem has more accessibility tooling (react-aria), component libraries (Radix UI), and SSR maturity (Next.js). Team ramp-up faster with React.

alternatives_considered.name: Java/Spring Boot instead of Node.js/NestJS for backend
alternatives_considered.summary: Use Java Spring Boot for API gateway and business logic services
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Viable alternative. Rejected for this engagement because: (1) Node.js has better IBM i Access client libraries (node-jt400), (2) TypeScript across full stack reduces context switching, (3) NestJS provides Spring-like structure with lighter runtime. Java would be preferred if team had strong Java expertise.
